name: org.machanism.machai:bindex-core
description: Bindex Core provides Java 8 compatible core functionality for Bindex metadata management in the Machanism ecosystem. It manages structured org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex records that describe reusable projects, libraries, integrations, dependencies, classifications, constructors, features, customization points, examples, and repository coordinates. The library supports generation, registration, persistence, retrieval, library recommendation, dependency traversal, and semantic project assembly workflows. Public APIs in org.machanism.machai.bindex.core provide repository abstractions, MongoDB-backed storage, vector-search retrieval, prompt classification, embedding generation, and selection of matching libraries from natural-language requirements. Public APIs in org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools expose the same capabilities as GenAI function tools so LLM-assisted applications can retrieve Bindex metadata, select relevant libraries, register bindex.json files, and register Bindex JSON objects at runtime. The module stores serialized Bindex JSON payloads together with searchable fields such as logical id, name, version, languages, domains, layers, integrations, and classification embeddings in MongoDB. It uses configured GenAI providers for request classification and configured embedding providers for vector representations, then queries MongoDB vector search with optional score thresholds and result limits. Runtime configuration includes BINDEX_REPO_URL, BINDEX_USER, BINDEX_PASSWORD, embedding.model, pick.model, gw.model, and pick.score. This artifact is intended for applications, build automation, AI agents, and tool-calling workflows that need a searchable registry of reusable component metadata and semantic library selection from descriptive requirements.

usageContext: Register bindex.json metadata documents for reusable libraries and projects
Persist Bindex records and classification embeddings in MongoDB
Retrieve Bindex records by unique id
Recommend libraries from natural-language project requirements using GenAI classification and vector similarity
Expose Bindex operations as function tools for LLM-assisted workflows
Resolve dependency identifiers recursively for registered Bindex entries
Support project assembly workflows that select components from semantic metadata rather than exact coordinates

constructors.description: Creates a Picker backed by the configured Bindex repository and configuration. Picker orchestrates Bindex registration, prompt classification, embedding generation, MongoDB vector search, lookup, and dependency traversal.

examples.description: Creates a MongoDB-backed repository and passes it with a Configurator to a Picker so the application can register Bindex records and perform semantic library selection. Configure MongoDB access with BINDEX_REPO_URL, BINDEX_USER, and BINDEX_PASSWORD before constructing the repository.

constructors.description: Creates a MongoDB-backed repository for persisting and retrieving Bindex documents. The repository stores serialized Bindex JSON, searchable metadata fields, and classification embedding vectors used by vector search.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator = ...;
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository repository = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository(700, configurator);
examples.description: Initializes repository access for embeddings with 700 dimensions. Use this when an application needs direct repository operations such as save, getBindex, or find.

constructors.description: Creates a function-tool provider that registers Bindex-related tools for GenAI workflows. The tools include get_bindex, pick_libraries, register_bindex, and register_bindex_json.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools();
// Register tools with the MachAI GenAI/tool framework used by the application.
examples.description: Constructs the AI tool adapter so supported GenAI integrations can fetch Bindex metadata, recommend libraries, and register Bindex documents through function calls.

features.name: save
features.description: Saves a Bindex entry using Picker. The method generates a classification embedding for the Bindex record and stores the entry through the configured repository. It returns the unique identifier assigned to the saved Bindex entry and throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the embedding cannot be generated.

examples.description: Registers a prepared Bindex object and stores its embedding in MongoDB. Ensure embedding.model is configured so the Picker can create the vector representation.

features.name: pick
features.signature: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> pick(java.lang.String prompt, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Recommends registered Bindex entries from a natural-language prompt and minimum score. Picker classifies the prompt using a configured GenAI provider, generates an embedding for the classification, and queries the Bindex repository for matching entries using vector search and metadata filters.

examples.description: Finds registered libraries that semantically match a project requirement. Configure pick.model or gw.model for classification and embedding.model for embedding generation.

features.name: getBindex
features.description: Retrieves a Bindex entry by its unique identifier through the repository contract. Returns the Bindex entry when found or null when no record exists for the supplied id.

examples.description: Loads a previously registered Bindex document by id. This is useful when a build tool, AI agent, or application already knows the exact artifact coordinate.

features.name: find
features.signature: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> find(org.machanism.machai.schema.Classification[] classifications, java.util.List<java.lang.Double> embedding, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator config)
features.description: Finds Bindex records that match classification and embedding criteria. The repository performs vector similarity search with supplied embeddings, search limits, score thresholds, and configuration context.

examples.description: Runs a lower-level repository search when the caller already has classifications and an embedding vector. Most applications should use Picker.pick unless they need direct repository control.

features.name: getBindex
features.description: AI function-tool operation that retrieves Bindex metadata for a given project or library id. Throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the Bindex is not found and may throw com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonProcessingException during result serialization.

examples.description: Invokes the tool adapter directly to retrieve metadata. In a GenAI runtime, this same method is exposed as the get_bindex function tool.

features.name: getRecommendedLibraries
features.signature: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> getRecommendedLibraries(java.lang.String prompt, java.lang.Double score, int vectorSearchLimits, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: AI function-tool operation that recommends libraries based on a user prompt, score threshold, vector search limit, and configuration. It enables LLM-assisted workflows to select reusable components dynamically from project requirements.

examples.description: Uses the pick_libraries tool behavior from Java code. If score is not supplied in tool workflows, the default can come from pick.score configuration.

features.name: registerBindex
features.signature: java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.String> registerBindex(java.lang.String fileName, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: AI function-tool operation that registers a Bindex record from a project-local JSON file. The fileName defaults to bindex.json in tool usage and must exist within the supplied project directory.

examples.description: Registers a bindex.json file from the current project directory and returns a map containing the created record id or an error message.

features.name: registerBindexJson
features.description: AI function-tool operation that registers a Bindex record supplied directly as an org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex object rather than reading it from a file.

examples.description: Registers an in-memory Bindex object. This is useful when an AI workflow builds the metadata object dynamically and does not need an intermediate file.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository
customizations.description: Repository abstraction for managing Bindex records. Developers can provide alternate implementations to store Bindex metadata in systems other than MongoDB while preserving Picker behavior for save, lookup, and recommendation operations.

examples.description: Shows the shape of a custom repository implementation for applications that want to back Bindex storage with another persistence technology while reusing Picker orchestration.

customizations.name: Runtime configuration properties
customizations.description: Bindex Core behavior is customized through runtime configuration. MongoDB access can be configured with BINDEX_REPO_URL, BINDEX_USER, and BINDEX_PASSWORD. Embedding generation uses embedding.model. Request classification uses pick.model when present, otherwise gw.model. Tool-based recommendations can use pick.score as the default minimum relevance threshold.

examples.snippet:
BINDEX_REPO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017
BINDEX_USER=bindex_user
BINDEX_PASSWORD=secret
embedding.model=text-embedding-model
pick.model=classification-model
pick.score=0.85
examples.description: Example configuration values for connecting to MongoDB, selecting GenAI models, and setting a default recommendation score.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository
studs.description: Repository interface for managing Bindex records. It defines operations for retrieving a Bindex by id, saving a Bindex with an embedding vector, and finding recommended entries from classifications, embeddings, search limits, score thresholds, and configuration. Implement this interface to plug in alternate persistence or search backends.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository repository = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository(700, configurator);
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker picker = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker(repository, configurator);
examples.description: Uses the provided MongoDB implementation through the repository interface so Picker remains decoupled from the concrete storage implementation.

examples.title: Install Bindex Core as a Maven dependency
examples.description: Add the library to a Maven project so Java code can register and retrieve Bindex records. Because this artifact is part of the Machanism MachAI ecosystem, use the repository and version appropriate for your Machanism distribution.
steps: Add org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT to the application pom.xml dependencies.
Ensure the Machanism artifacts are available from the configured Maven repositories.
Configure MongoDB and GenAI model settings before using Picker or BindexFunctionTools.
examples.snippet:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.machanism.machai</groupId>
  <artifactId>bindex-core</artifactId>
  <version>1.2.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

examples.title: Configure runtime services
examples.description: Bindex Core requires MongoDB for repository persistence and configured GenAI providers for prompt classification and embedding generation. MongoDB settings identify the repository, while embedding.model, pick.model, gw.model, and pick.score control semantic selection behavior.
steps: Set BINDEX_REPO_URL to the MongoDB connection URL used for Bindex persistence.
Set BINDEX_USER and BINDEX_PASSWORD if authentication is required.
Set embedding.model to the embedding provider used to generate classification vectors.
Set pick.model for classification, or rely on gw.model when pick.model is not present.
Optionally set pick.score to control the default minimum recommendation relevance score.
examples.snippet:
BINDEX_REPO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017
BINDEX_USER=bindex_user
BINDEX_PASSWORD=secret
embedding.model=text-embedding-model
pick.model=classification-model
gw.model=default-gateway-model
pick.score=0.85

examples.title: Register a Bindex object programmatically
examples.description: Use Picker to save an org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex object. Picker generates and stores the classification embedding together with the serialized Bindex JSON and searchable metadata fields.
steps: Create or load an org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex object.
Create org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository with the embedding dimension count and Configurator.
Create org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker with the repository and Configurator.
Call picker.save(bindex) and store the returned record id.

examples.title: Pick libraries from a natural-language requirement
examples.description: Use Picker to recommend registered Bindex records for a project requirement. The prompt is classified, converted to an embedding, and matched against MongoDB vector search results with score filtering.
steps: Configure MongoDB, embedding.model, and pick.model or gw.model.
Create a repository and Picker.
Call picker.pick with the requirement prompt, vector search limit, and minimum score.
Use returned org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo values to inspect recommended ids, descriptions, versions, and relevance scores.

examples.title: Use Bindex operations as AI function tools
examples.description: Applications that integrate MachAI tool-calling can expose BindexFunctionTools so LLM workflows can retrieve Bindex records, recommend libraries, register bindex.json files, and register in-memory Bindex JSON objects.
steps: Install the bindex-core dependency in the host application.
Configure MongoDB and GenAI models.
Create org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools and register it with the MachAI GenAI/tool framework used by the application.
Allow the model to invoke get_bindex, pick_libraries, register_bindex, or register_bindex_json during a workflow.

examples.title: Register a project-local bindex.json file through the tool adapter
examples.description: When a project contains a bindex.json file, BindexFunctionTools can read and register it from the project directory, returning the created record id.
steps: Create a valid bindex.json file in the project directory.
Create BindexFunctionTools and provide the project directory as java.io.File.
Call registerBindex with bindex.json, the project directory, and Configurator.
Check the returned map for the registered record id.
